focus: finale scaffold code-vs-canonical-reference correctness (IQN, Dabney et al. ICML 2018, arXiv:1806.06923)
summary: No correctness bugs found in the IQN scaffold versus the canonical reference. Verified: (1) cosine embedding cos(pi*i*tau) i=1..64 + Linear(64,512)+ReLU; (2) Hadamard tau-modulation psi*phi (not concat); (3) pairwise TD tensor [B,N,N'] with quantile weights on the correct prediction-level axis; (4) loss = mean over target j, sum over prediction i, batch mean; (5) forward() returns per-action K-sample mean Q for eval-argmax; (6) greedy/target use K=32-sample mean; (7) parameter budget consistent (head Linear(512,n_actions)+Linear(64,512) embedding, removing QR-DQN's 512x200 output block). Non-blocking note: target-net-only next-action selection is canonical risk-neutral IQN (not Double-IQN), matching the stated design and the QR-DQN baseline's target construction.
